title: Holopogon microglottis
taxonomy:
Holopogon microglottis belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. 
This plant is classified as a species of fern, specifically within the genus Holopogon. 
The classification of Holopogon microglottis is further categorized under the class Polypodiopsida. 
It is native to Australia and New Zealand, and its classification as a species is recognized in various botanical sources. 
The classification system used for Holopogon microglottis follows the principles of the International Code of Botanical Nomenclature.
uses: Holopogon microglottis, also known as the small-throated holopogon, has been reported to have various traditional medicinal uses in some cultures. Its leaves and stems have been used in herbal remedies to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties are also believed to contribute to its medicinal value. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Holopogon microglottis for medicinal purposes. As with any plant-based remedy, caution and proper identification are essential when using Holopogon microglottis for medicinal uses.

intro: Holopogon microglottis, also known as Mountain Sage, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to North America, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues and soothing skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Holopogon microglottis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Mountain Sage include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
